insight.summary: Microsoft Graph API: OneDrive upload-by-path creates files at root, not in-place
insight.detail: `PUT /me/drive/root:/{filename}:/content` always creates or overwrites a file at the drive root, regardless of where the original file lives. For download-modify-upload workflows (e.g. editing a PowerPoint in a subfolder), this silently creates a duplicate at root instead of updating the original. Use `PUT /me/drive/items/{item-id}/content` to update a file in place by its ID.
insight.action: When updating an existing OneDrive file's content, use the item ID endpoint (`/me/drive/items/{id}/content`) rather than the path-based endpoint (`/me/drive/root:/{path}:/content`).
